source_pr.title: pkg/executor: fix flaky query watch test
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #61049

Problem Summary:
- `TestQueryWatch` was flaky due to fixed sleeps racing with async quarantine propagation.

### What changed and how does it work?
- Replace `time.Sleep` with `require.Eventually` and verify the expected MySQL error code deterministically.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)

Test Details:
- `make failpoint-enable && (go test -run ^TestQueryWatch$ --tags=intest ./pkg/executor/internal/querywatch; rc=$?; make failpoint-disable; exit $rc)`
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: pkg/executor: fix flaky query watch test
failure_signature: pkg/executor: fix flaky query watch test
root_cause_explanation: The query-watch quarantine/removal effect is asynchronous, but the test previously used a fixed 1-second sleep and then asserted a specific error, which can race on slow/loaded CI. This PR switches to `require.Eventually` and checks the returned SQL error code, so the test waits until the expected quarantine error is actually observable.
fix_pattern: Add polling/wait (`Eventually`) for async conditions/cleanup to reduce timing-related flakiness.

evidence: evidence: pkg/executor: fix flaky query watch test
